jokes.text: It's over eight million bucks. That's a lot of deniros.
jokes.setup: Character references a large sum of money in criminal context
jokes.punchline: Uses 'deniros' as slang for money, referencing Robert De Niro's gangster roles
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits the mafia character perfectly, economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh, this 'a Berry Berry bad!
jokes.setup: Mafia robot reacting to bad news about money
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated Italian-American accent stereotype applied to robot
jokes.explanation: Standard character comedy, fits the robot mafia archetype well

jokes.text: Earth's boring. I'll save us. Let's go visit my parents at their fabulous casino in Mars Vegas.
jokes.setup: Characters are bored and need entertainment
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of parents owning a casino on Mars Vegas
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's privileged background casually

jokes.text: Look out, penny slots. I've got a system. It's to put all my money in you.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claims to have a gambling system
jokes.punchline: His 'system' is just to lose all his money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - confident delivery of terrible logic, great structure

jokes.text: What? It's full of fat guys in sandals.
jokes.setup: Amy warns Vegas isn't good for 'people like him'
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg misunderstands, thinks she means his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character misunderstanding, Zoidberg's obliviousness to his actual problems

jokes.text: Oh, yes, better. A lonely weekend in my dumpster with a jar of pennies and tears. Sounds good. See you Monday!
jokes.setup: Friends suggest Zoidberg stay behind from Vegas trip
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg cheerfully describes his pathetic alternative plans
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy, Zoidberg's upbeat delivery of depressing content

jokes.text: Better ditch the loot somewhere. Hey-yo, how about that dumpster down there? You mean the one in that alley off 72nd street? Yeah. What do you think I'm pointing at?
jokes.setup: Criminals need to hide stolen money quickly
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific location for something they're pointing at
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor, overly specific details in casual conversation

jokes.text: God, it's Zoidberg. I hate to bother you, but... Okay, okay, I'll shut up!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to get attention from departing friends
jokes.punchline: Gives up immediately when they drive away without stopping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's resignation to being ignored

jokes.text: This very lucky casino own. Someone wins one million dollar every hour! Usually it us.
jokes.setup: Casino owner brags about frequent big winners
jokes.punchline: Reveals the house always wins anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic casino owner logic, good structure with misdirection

jokes.text: Well, they do work for minimum wage in a land that was once theirs. Yeah, but they never made wise use of the land.
jokes.setup: Fry points out the injustice of native Martians' situation
jokes.punchline: Wong dismisses it with typical colonizer logic
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered through character obliviousness

jokes.text: When my ancestor Reginald wong found them here, they had no bingo parlors and only one prostitute.
jokes.setup: Wong explains why natives didn't use land wisely
jokes.punchline: His metrics for civilization are gambling and prostitution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of colonial justification rhetoric with casino owner logic

jokes.text: Pfft. Pathetic.
jokes.setup: Wong's judgment of native Martian 'inadequacy'
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reaction to his own ridiculous standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect punctuation to the previous joke, economical character moment

jokes.text: Ooh, perfect timing! It's the weekly changing of the shrimp!
jokes.setup: Characters arrive at buffet
jokes.punchline: Timing coincides with replacing week-old seafood (with flies buzzing)
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup, plays on casino buffet stereotypes

jokes.text: It's Mars' happiest place on earth!
jokes.setup: Wong showing off the casino's money room
jokes.punchline: Says 'place on earth' while they're on Mars
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism, fits Wong's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: This much money could feed a small, starving planet. But it doesn't!
jokes.setup: Leela observes the vast wealth could solve world hunger
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully notes that it doesn't
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark comedy, excellent structure and economy, captures wealth inequality absurdity

jokes.text: Don't mess with blind Joe. Nothing get past his sense of smell. Yeah, that's why he in here. Guarding galaxy's most secure safe.
jokes.setup: Introduction of blind security guard with super-smell
jokes.punchline: His qualification for guarding money is smelling ability
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic for security system, moderately funny character introduction

jokes.text: This whole place is finely tuned machine for separating suckers from their money.
jokes.setup: Wong explaining casino operations
jokes.punchline: Bluntly honest about casino's predatory nature
jokes.explanation: Standard casino owner honesty joke, fits character but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Category-five sucker now arriving.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg arrives at casino with money
jokes.punchline: Casino uses hurricane classification system for gambling marks
jokes.explanation: Clever meteorological metaphor for casino operations, very economical

jokes.text: ♪ the minute you walked in the joint / I could smell you were a man who was stinkin' ♪
jokes.setup: Parody of classic lounge song for Zoidberg's arrival
jokes.punchline: References his literal bad smell instead of romantic appeal
jokes.explanation: Good song parody, subverts romantic trope with Zoidberg's grossness

jokes.text: God didn't get to be God by giving away money.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claims God gave him money
jokes.punchline: Leela applies business logic to divine generosity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cynical theology, perfect economy and structure, very quotable

jokes.text: This is why you never see a poor person with millions of dollars
jokes.setup: Zoidberg immediately gambling away his windfall
jokes.punchline: Tautological observation delivered as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfectly circular logic delivered as wisdom, excellent deadpan observation

jokes.text: I don't know. I think it's called roulette.
jokes.setup: Amy asks what he's doing gambling
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's uncertainty about basic casino game while playing it
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment showing his cluelessness about what he's doing

jokes.text: Vodka with a sardine in it, please. In fact, vodka with a sardine all around!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg celebrating his big win
jokes.punchline: Orders disgusting cocktail for everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg generosity with his terrible taste in food/drinks

jokes.text: No! I'm having fun and a sardini!
jokes.setup: Amy tells him to be sensible with winnings
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg creates portmanteau of 'sardine' and 'martini'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay callback to his gross drink choice, good character consistency

jokes.text: Now that's the God I know.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg loses everything on next bet
jokes.punchline: Leela's cynical theology confirmed
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure, validates Leela's earlier cynicism perfectly

jokes.text: But for one brief moment, I made everybody on Mars happy. And if you ask me, that makes me a winner.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg lost fortune, Amy criticizes him
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg finds positive spin on complete financial disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg delusion, finding victory in complete failure, very in-character

jokes.text: Category-five loser now departing.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg leaves casino broke
jokes.punchline: PA system updates his classification from 'sucker' to 'loser'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with escalation, tracking Zoidberg's casino journey

jokes.text: This Judith Krantz novel's only missing its frontispiece.
jokes.setup: Clamps needs to find least broken object to hit Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Evaluates romance novel's condition with surprising literary knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion - violent thug with unexpected literary sophistication

jokes.text: He can't be lying', boss not after a krantzing like that.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg hit with Judith Krantz novel
jokes.punchline: Joey turns author's name into verb for book-beating
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay callback, turning proper noun into violence verb

jokes.text: He's full of ink! Whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo, whoo.
jokes.setup: They attack Zoidberg in dumpster
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg releases ink cloud like octopus, Joey makes train sounds
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup for later invisibility plot, Joey's silly sounds

jokes.text: We should have brought a gun.
jokes.setup: Mafia completely fails to kill Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Boss realizes obvious oversight in organized crime planning
jokes.explanation: Good understatement, reveals incompetence of robot mafia

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Amy's mirror got a tattoo!
jokes.setup: Characters checking for Vegas consequences
jokes.punchline: Amy thinks her reflection's tattoo is on the mirror
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag, Amy's momentary confusion about reflections

jokes.text: Oh, Hermes, you and your preposterous Sci-Fi nonsense. No, this is simply a translucidating ink polarizer.
jokes.setup: Hermes suggests it's a tattoo removal laser
jokes.punchline: Professor dismisses sci-fi while describing even more sci-fi device
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment, rejecting simple explanation for complex technobabble

jokes.text: Nobody said that.
jokes.setup: Hermes assumes new device is less painful
jokes.punchline: Professor clarifies it's probably more painful
jokes.explanation: Good Professor callousness, perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: No, no, no. Not in any sense of the word. But essentially, yes, entirely.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks if he's invisible
jokes.punchline: Professor contradicts himself completely within one sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Professor contradiction, perfect structure showing his scattered thinking

jokes.text: We're, like, 85% sure you're in there!
jokes.setup: Mafia looking for Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific confidence percentage in uncertain situation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist precision in imprecise situation, fits robot character

jokes.text: Zoidberg's not here.
jokes.setup: Invisible Zoidberg trying to hide from mafia
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person while obviously present
jokes.explanation: Classic invisible person trope, good Zoidberg logic

jokes.text: there's some sort of smelly air pocket over here.
jokes.setup: Mafia leaving, Clamps notices smell
jokes.punchline: Describes Zoidberg's location without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character traits, near-miss detection

jokes.text: What are you, an idiot? Pretty much, yeah.
jokes.setup: Boss insults Joey's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Joey honestly confirms his own stupidity
jokes.explanation: Standard but well-executed character dynamic, good timing

jokes.text: Then you won't notice when I claim your idea as my own.
jokes.setup: Joey admits he's an idiot
jokes.punchline: Boss plans to exploit Joey's stupidity immediately
jokes.explanation: Good boss exploitation of underling, logical follow-up to previous joke

jokes.text: Okay, fine, I just retire to my fabulous mansion.
jokes.setup: Mafia demands his casino
jokes.punchline: Wong casually gives up, mentions his backup wealth
jokes.explanation: Good wealthy character moment, casual attitude toward major loss

jokes.text: Who do you think been paying your rent? What's rent?
jokes.setup: Mrs. Wong reveals she pays Amy's rent
jokes.punchline: Amy doesn't know what rent is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy privilege moment, shows her complete disconnect from financial reality

jokes.text: It's not a dump. It's the company housing we built for our employees.
jokes.setup: Characters arrive at what looks like a dump
jokes.punchline: Wong reveals it's actually employee housing
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary on worker exploitation, perfect misdirection

jokes.text: You can't afford tissue any more. This is Walgreens brand nose paper.
jokes.setup: Amy uses what she thinks is tissue
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg reveals it's generic store-brand substitute
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor, specific brand reference adds realism to their downfall

jokes.text: No, you snuck in here while I was grabbing a bubble cake.
jokes.setup: Amy says Zoidberg snuck in while she bathed
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg claims Amy invaded his bubble-eating space
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg gross habit reveal, 'bubble cake' is absurd and disgusting

jokes.text: Of course, because no one can possibly know what I'm up to.
jokes.setup: Amy explains they'll use his invisibility for heist
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg is completely visible to audience while saying this
jokes.explanation: Good visual irony for animation, Zoidberg's confidence contrasts with visibility

jokes.text: Um, how come we've never been in this room before? I think this chart will answer that.
jokes.setup: Fry notices they've never seen this room
jokes.punchline: Amy points to chart that presumably explains convenient room appearance
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about convenient plot rooms appearing in TV shows

jokes.text: Over here with the donut.
jokes.setup: Everyone discussing if plan will work
jokes.punchline: Invisible Zoidberg reveals location by eating during meeting
jokes.explanation: Good invisible character gag, Zoidberg's eating habits give him away

jokes.text: He stinks like the inside of a tauntaun.
jokes.setup: Discussing Zoidberg's smell problem for heist
jokes.punchline: References Star Wars creature's presumably awful interior
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference, specific and vivid description of bad smell

jokes.text: It ain't my gambling diaper. I just changed it.
jokes.setup: Characters wondering about bad smell
jokes.punchline: Patron rules out his gambling diaper as source
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail about gambling addiction, perfectly casual delivery

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm just so hungry. Don't spoil your appetite. You need to eat 300 pounds of cash salad.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg eating during heist prep
jokes.punchline: Amy calls the money he needs to eat 'cash salad'
jokes.explanation: Good food metaphor for money, fits Amy's planning style

jokes.text: We count on that for our job that we do.
jokes.setup: Workers' shrimp cart is damaged
jokes.punchline: Overly formal description of basic job dependency
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from overly formal speech pattern about simple work

jokes.text: I'm a cr-razy Texas robot millionaire, and I plan on losing billions in your 'casiny.'
jokes.setup: Bender creating distraction as rich gambler
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated accent and mispronounces 'casino'
jokes.explanation: Good Bender disguise attempt, exaggerated character performance

jokes.text: Nelson, thank God you're here, Hermes!
jokes.setup: Hermes disguised as Nigerian prince
jokes.punchline: Breaks character immediately when recognized
jokes.explanation: Good disguise failure comedy, references email scam trope

jokes.text: It means, uh, 'we must leave now in an unusual manner.'
jokes.setup: Hermes makes up fake foreign phrase meaning
jokes.punchline: Translation perfectly describes their current situation
jokes.explanation: Good improvisation humor, fake translation accidentally describes reality

jokes.text: Everybody say 'fresh shrimp!' Oh... that was a rough ride.
jokes.setup: Group photo opportunity during escape
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg complains about elevator while burping money
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy moment, Zoidberg's priorities during crisis

jokes.text: Look! The shrimp cart's paying out!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg burping up money as they escape
jokes.punchline: Gamblers think the food cart is a slot machine hitting jackpot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual misdirection, gamblers' addiction makes them see jackpots everywhere

jokes.text: Keep going. We're almost outside! Zoidberg, try to just burp up singles.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg burping money during escape
jokes.punchline: Amy tries to manage the denominations he's vomiting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd situation, Amy's practical crisis management

jokes.text: Smell with your eyes what I see with my nose.
jokes.setup: Blind Joe detecting the thieves
jokes.punchline: Mixes up sensory metaphors due to his blindness
jokes.explanation: Clever sensory confusion from blind character, good callback to his abilities

jokes.text: And let this be a lesson to any other albino lobsters thinking of robbing our casino
jokes.setup: Boss about to execute Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Warning directed at very specific demographic of potential thieves
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific threat, assumes there's a whole demographic of albino lobster criminals

jokes.text: This casino is wrongfully ours.
jokes.setup: Learning casino should belong to Martians
jokes.punchline: Boss claims it's 'wrongfully' theirs (instead of rightfully)
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism acknowledging their illegitimate claim while asserting it

jokes.text: So what happens to the hero of our story, Zoidberg? Will I be invisible forever? No, only until you take a bath and wash off this invisible ink. So, forever.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks about his invisibility duration
jokes.punchline: Professor says until he bathes, Zoidberg confirms he'll never bathe
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and character consistency, perfect callback to established trait

jokes.text: So, forever.
jokes.setup: Professor saying invisibility will last until he takes a bath
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's matter-of-fact admission he'll never bathe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg self-awareness about his hygiene habits delivered with resigned acceptance
